machinery_genes.quote: These putative T6SS loci contain homologs of 12 core T6SS genes... herein, T6SS genes are referred to by their generic names or by the proposed nomenclature of Shalom
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tssA = A1S_1308 (COG3515) in A. baumannii ATCC 17978, listed among the 12 conserved core T6SS components of this strain's syntenic cluster.

machinery_genes.quote: The AAA protein ClpV utilizes ATP hydrolysis in order to dissemble another T6SS tubular structure composed of interacting TssB/TssC proteins... TssB/TssC tubules, which are critical for a functional T6SS, are homologous to the tail sheath of T4 bacteriophage
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013; named in text and Table 1: tssB = A1S_1293/A1S_1294 (COG3516) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.quote: another T6SS tubular structure composed of interacting TssB/TssC proteins... TssB/TssC tubules, which are critical for a functional T6SS, are homologous to the tail sheath of T4 bacteriophage
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013; named in text and Table 1: tssC = A1S_1295 (COG3517) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.role: secreted hexameric inner tube (T6SS activity marker)
machinery_genes.quote: T6SSs incorporate characteristic components, including the secreted proteins Hcp and VgrG... Hcp forms hexamers that assemble as tubular structures and resemble the bacteriophage T4 tail tube
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013 characterise Hcp directly in ATCC 17978 (purified, antibody raised, secretion shown T6SS-dependent). Table 1: tssD/hcp = A1S_1296. This is one of the instance's listed proteins but counts as machinery (secreted tube) per the T6SS brief.

machinery_genes.quote: These putative T6SS loci contain homologs of 12 core T6SS genes... herein, T6SS genes are referred to by their generic names or by the proposed nomenclature of Shalom
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tssE = A1S_1297 (COG3518) in ATCC 17978, among the 12 conserved core components.

machinery_genes.quote: These putative T6SS loci contain homologs of 12 core T6SS genes... herein, T6SS genes are referred to by their generic names or by the proposed nomenclature of Shalom
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tssF = A1S_1298/A1S_1299 (COG3519) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.quote: These putative T6SS loci contain homologs of 12 core T6SS genes... herein, T6SS genes are referred to by their generic names or by the proposed nomenclature of Shalom
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tssG = A1S_1300 (COG3520) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.quote: structural proteins ClpV, TssM, and TssL... The AAA protein ClpV utilizes ATP hydrolysis in order to dissemble another T6SS tubular structure composed of interacting TssB/TssC proteins
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013; named in text and Table 1: tssH/clpV = A1S_1307 (COG0542) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.quote: T6SSs incorporate characteristic components, including the secreted proteins Hcp and VgrG... VgrG proteins, some of which have evolved to contain virulence activity in their C-termini, are structurally similar to the puncturing device of T4 bacteriophage
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013; Table 1 lists tssI/vgrG copies for ATCC 17978 as A1S_0550, A1S_1288, A1S_1289, A1S_3364 (multiple VgrG paralogs, several outside the main cluster). Counted as machinery (spike) per the T6SS brief; VgrGs with evolved C-terminal toxin domains would be effector cargo but the structural VgrG core is apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: These putative T6SS loci contain homologs of 12 core T6SS genes... herein, T6SS genes are referred to by their generic names or by the proposed nomenclature of Shalom
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tssK = A1S_1309 (COG3522) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.quote: structural proteins ClpV, TssM, and TssL... TssM and TssL, homologs of the T4SS IcmF and DotU proteins, respectively, physically interact, and are required for secretion of conserved T6SS components
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013; named in text and Table 1: tssL = A1S_1310 (COG3455) in ATCC 17978.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane complex (large, IcmF-like); essential for Hcp secretion
machinery_genes.quote: its ability to secrete the conserved component Hcp is dependent upon at least one other gene in the cluster... TssM, a structural component of the T6SS, has been shown to be required for T6SS activity, and is therefore required for Hcp secretion
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013 directly characterise TssM in ATCC 17978 via deletion (delta-tssM abolishes Hcp secretion, complemented by plasmid). Table 1: tssM = A1S_1302/A1S_1303 (COG3523). Highest-confidence machinery call (functional knockout in this strain).

machinery_genes.quote: The gene clusters encode the hallmarks... as well as accessory components and genes with unknown function
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tagF = A1S_1304 (COG3913) in ATCC 17978, listed in the syntenic cluster as an accessory T6SS gene.

machinery_genes.quote: The gene clusters encode the hallmarks... as well as accessory components and genes with unknown function
machinery_genes.note: Weber et al 2013, Table 1: tagN = A1S_1305 (COG2885) in ATCC 17978, listed in the cluster as an accessory T6SS gene.

notes: The A. baumannii ATCC 17978 T6SS is a single syntenic core locus (A1S_1293-A1S_1310) characterised gene-by-gene by Weber et al 2013 (PLoS ONE), the founding genomic+functional study. That paper names the 12 conserved core genes by the unified Tss/Tag nomenclature and gives ATCC 17978 locus tags in Table 1: tssB (A1S_1293/1294), tssC (A1S_1295), tssD/hcp (A1S_1296), tssE (A1S_1297), tssF (A1S_1298/1299), tssG (A1S_1300), tssM (A1S_1302/1303), tagF (A1S_1304), tagN (A1S_1305), tssH/clpV (A1S_1307), tssA (A1S_1308), tssK (A1S_1309), tssL (A1S_1310); plus tssI/vgrG paralogs outside the main cluster (A1S_0550, A1S_1288, A1S_1289, A1S_3364). Hcp and TssM are functionally validated in this exact strain (Hcp purified + antibody; tssM deletion abolishes Hcp secretion). Per the T6SS brief, Hcp (the instance's listed A1S_1296) and VgrG are counted as machinery (secreted tube/spike), not effectors. TssJ is included as an entry only to record the paper's explicit statement that the ATCC 17978 cluster LACKS a TssJ outer-membrane lipoprotein homolog; it is reported absent from this genome and should not be expected as a recoverable gene. PAAR is NOT listed: no consulted paper names a PAAR-repeat protein for this strain's T6SS, so it is omitted under the no-padding rule. Deliberately excluded: delivered toxin effectors and immunity proteins (e.g. the d-Lys / PG-editing immunity work of Le et al 2020 PMC7439305 and the Tse4 effector of Le et al 2021 PMC8501793, which is the antagonistic-warfare context for this instance's curated effectors), and the periplasmic racemase RacK (ACX60_11360, a PG-editing immunity factor, not apparatus). The d-amino-acid / Tse-effector papers are cited as effector-context only; they do not add named structural apparatus genes beyond Weber 2013. All quotes verbatim from the Weber 2013 PMC full text (PMC3554697); Table-1-derived calls flagged in their notes.
